2026-04-17T08:15:32.4431722Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T08:15:32.4432182Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T08:15:32.4432545Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T08:15:32.4432859Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T08:15:32.4433101Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T08:15:32.4433263Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-17T08:18:54.8475867Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T08:16:40.860275249Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58272,\"title\":\"[GeneratorAgent] Docs: Add MPG migration Copilot Chat example to README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.GeneratorAgent`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n**Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (#58169) by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58169 separated the previously unified `sdk-migration` skill into two distinct skills:\\n- `.github/skills/dpg-migration/SKILL.md` — data-plane migration workflow\\n- `.github/skills/mpg-migration/SKILL.md` — management-plane migration workflow (newly added)\\n\\nThe `README.md` was updated to reference both skills throughout, but the **Examples** section\\u0026#39;s \\u0026#34;Use with Copilot Chat\\u0026#34; subsection was not updated to include an MPG migration example.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **`Examples → Use with Copilot Chat`** section only shows how to invoke `dpg-migration` via Copilot Chat prompt, with no equivalent example for `mpg-migration`\\n- The MPG migration skill has different required inputs (targets `Azure.ResourceManager.*` packages; spec repo path is optional since it defaults to `../azure-rest-api-specs`) and the prompt pattern should be documented in parallel with the DPG example\\n- The \\u0026#34;Use with a migration skill\\u0026#34; subsection describes a generic workflow that pre-dates the split; it could call out the setup-phase difference between DPG and MPG (MPG uses `RegenSdkLocal.ps1` rather than `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nIn the `## Examples` section, the `### Use with Copilot Chat` subsection currently ends after the DPG example. Add an MPG migration subsection immediately after the closing paragraph of the DPG section:\\n\\n````markdown\\nFor management-plane libraries (`Azure.ResourceManager.*`), use the `mpg-migration` skill:\\n\\n```\\nUse the mpg-migration skill to migrate (repo-root)/sdk/(service)/Azure.ResourceManager.(Service)\\nThe local specs repo is at (specs-root)/specification/(service)/(spec-directory)\\n```\\n\\nFor example:\\n\\n```\\nUse the mpg-migration skill to migrate C:\\\\git\\\\azure-sdk-for-net\\\\sdk\\\\compute\\\\Azure.ResourceManager.Compute\\nThe local specs repo is at C:\\\\git\\\\azure-rest-api-specs\\\\specification\\\\compute\\\\resource-manager\\n```\\n\\nIf the specs repo is already at the default sibling path (`../azure-rest-api-specs`), you can omit the path:\\n\\n```\\nUse the mpg-migration skill to migrate C:\\\\git\\\\azure-sdk-for-net\\\\sdk\\\\compute\\\\Azure.ResourceManager.Compute\\n```\\n````\\n\\nAlso update the `### Use with a migration skill` subsection to briefly note the setup-phase difference between DPG and MPG. Replace the current generic workflow list:\\n\\n```markdown\\nThe tools are designed to be called by the dedicated migration skills. Use [`dpg-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/dpg-migration/SKILL.md) for data-plane libraries and [`mpg-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mpg-migration/SKILL.md) for management-plane libraries. The workflows share the same build-fix loop but differ in setup:\\n\\n**DPG (data-plane) workflow:**\\n1. **Setup** — `pregen_cleanup` → `validate_tsp_config` → `commit_iteration` → `run_code_generation`\\n2. **Build-fix loop** — `build_and_classify` → `batch_fix` (for deterministic errors) → LLM reasoning (for non-deterministic errors) → rebuild\\n3. **Finalize** — `migrate_test_samples` → `run_tests` → `finalize_migration`\\n\\n**MPG (management-plane) workflow:**\\n1. **Setup** — Update `tsp-location.yaml` → `run_code_generation` (or `RegenSdkLocal.ps1`)\\n2. **Build-fix loop** — `build_and_classify` → `batch_fix` (deterministic) → spec-side decorator fixes → rebuild\\n3. **Breaking-change mitigation** — Export API → `batch_fix` → re-export → validate\\n4. **Finalize** — `finalize_migration` → `run_tests`\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo snippet changes required — this is pure prose documentation.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` already contains all required sections in the correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install/Build, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ⚠️ **Examples** — \\u0026#34;Use with Copilot Chat\\u0026#34; subsection is missing MPG example (this issue)\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 tools` — confirm no unexpected README changes\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
